mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase discount rates for equity valuations, leading to negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS rises month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate above 4.5% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending, causing immediate negative equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >55% of instances when UNRATE crosses the 4.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales combined with accelerating inflation indicates demand-pull price pressures that force monetary tightening, resulting in negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive changes in RSAFS and CPIAUCSL yield positive 20-day returns in >60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side contraction that outweighs demand, driving negative quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in >50% of periods when INDPRO declines during tightening phases.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly increases corporate earnings expectations, producing positive forward equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for GDPC1 against 20-day returns falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total employment and a falling unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, boosting consumer confidence and driving positive equity returns.
failure_condition: Combined positive PAYEMS and negative UNRATE changes yield negative 20-day returns in >55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 3.0% while inflation remains above 2.0%, real rates turn positive and compress equity valuations, causing short-term negative returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of periods meeting the threshold condition.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts during a post-shock recovery phase signals renewed capital investment and economic momentum, generating positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >50% of periods when HOUST increases during recovery regimes.
